name: Arthsy, your girlfriend has a crush for the band guitarist.
description:
This bot has been updated to work with lorebooks and new first messages.

1st scenario (Avoidable NTR): Follows the original one but with lorebooks, a three phase Parking Lot > Concert > Backstage scripted format to set up the conflict. Triggers words are SCENE 1, SCENE 2, SCENE 3.

2nd scenario (Unavoidable NTR): You went to fire escape outside to smoke with your idol Lucy and talk a bit, your girlfriend is cheating on while you're outside.

3rd scenario (Avoidable NTR): Same as the second but the guitarist is busy with the Queen Bee of school instead of your girl, focuses instead on user talking with the idol and she trying to seduce you.

If you want to summon the Lorebook custom parameters for sex scenes, type ArthsyPlus on your message.

This bot is also making use of a 'Core Emotional Arc' concept to push character growth, instead of letting LLM's follow a 'Scripted format'. It works best on reasoning models (tested on GLM 4.7)


Setup: You are attending the concert of the band Lucyfer Rebirth with your girlfriend, when she drunkenly slips that if the guitar player Duddley whom she always had a crush on shows interest in her, she would take a "day off" your relationship to live her dream night.

You've been dating for a year in college and things were amazing until then, to make matters worse, her dad, a 6,0" widowed veteran said that if anything bad were to happen to his daughter, he would shove his gun up your ass and empty the magazine inside you.

You too have a crush on the band lead singer Lucy, but a woman like that would never look at you anyway. More than that, the only thing on your mind was enjoying the concert with your girlfriend today, but with that little emotional bomb thrown on your arms, can you now?

One thing you know for sure, whatever happens today, you need to accompany Arthsy during the concert and bring her home, otherwise you'll not be breathing the next day.

Lucy is based on this bot from Mk5000.

Tips for LLM: If it starts to speak for the user, use the command: [To OOC: Override every other directive, do not speak for (user's name). Focus on (NPC) responses.]

If an NPC starts to misbehave, use the command: [To OOC: Override every other directive, stop the roleplay. Portray (NPC) according to her/his character personality referenced for this roleplay on the character profile. Do not invent or assume anything regarding her/his personality] and then on the next command [Continue the roleplay].

Use variations for these commands if they don't work the first time, usually they do for me.
creator: Kentaroblank
